# UC-22.14.72 · Session Authenticity — Token Replay Across Geographies

## Description

Detects same refresh token used from distant geolocations within short windows for SC-23 session authenticity.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm operation strings; (2) reduce noise with corporate travel; (3) integrate with IR-4; (4) force re-auth playbooks. Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:management` token events if available, or proxy OAuth logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm operation strings; (2) reduce noise with corporate travel; (3) integrate with IR-4; (4) force re-auth playbooks.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24d Operation="Token issued*"
| iplocation ClientIP
| stats earliest(_time) as t1, latest(_time) as t2, values(Country) as countries, values(ClientIP) as ips by UserId, SessionId
| eval dt=t2-t1
| where mvcount(countries)>1 AND dt < 7200
```

Understanding this SPL

**Session Authenticity — Token Replay Across Geographies (SC-23)** — Detects same refresh token used from distant geolocations within short windows for SC-23 session authenticity.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` token events if available, or proxy OAuth logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:management, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.

• `stats` aggregates events by **UserId, SessionId**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dt** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvcount(countries)>1 AND dt < 7200` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
Output fields: SessionId, UserId, countries, dt, ips, t1, t2.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (suspected replay), Map (IPs), Timeline

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24d Operation="Token issued*"
| iplocation ClientIP
| stats earliest(_time) as t1, latest(_time) as t2, values(Country) as countries, values(ClientIP) as ips by UserId, SessionId
| eval dt=t2-t1
| where mvcount(countries)>1 AND dt < 7200
```

## Visualization

Table (suspected replay), Map (IPs), Timeline. Key fields: `SessionId`, `UserId`, `countries`, `dt`, `ips`, `t1`.
